title: Same-lap choice expert via query-level ranking loss
summary: Public ranking docs emphasize that learning-to-rank works on grouped queries, and each lap naturally defines a query where several drivers are competing for a limited number of near-term pit decisions.
rationale: Public ranking docs emphasize that learning-to-rank works on grouped queries, and each lap naturally defines a query where several drivers are competing for a limited number of near-term pit decisions. This is more specific than generic classification and more concrete than prior generic ranking suggestions: train an explicit same-lap choice model with GroupId/qid=(Year,Race,LapNumber), then blend only its within-lap score ordering with the current global classifiers.
implementation_hint: Train one specialist with CatBoost QuerySoftMax or YetiRankPairwise, or XGBoost rank:ndcg / rank:pairwise, using per-lap groups. Keep labels binary, evaluate with standard OOF ROC AUC, and blend the expert mainly through within-query ranks or query-centered logits rather than raw probabilities.
expected_effect: Likely small-to-medium but orthogonal gain, especially if leaderboard/test rows differ more in field composition than in marginal feature distributions.
risk: Query objectives optimize within-group ordering, not calibrated probabilities; if most lap groups have very few positives, the model can become noisy unless groups are large enough and blend weight is conservative.
